prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sequence begins with a wide cityscape shot showing tall buildings, streets, and green areas. Over the city view is a large white “DOORDASH” logo with the phrase “Delicious, Delivered.” beneath it, plus small fine-print text along the bottom. It then cuts to a black frame.

After the black frame, the commercial shifts to a bright white studio setting with a vertical split-screen layout. On the left side, different people are shown eating foods topped or dipped with chocolate spread; on the right side, a jar labeled “HERSHEY’S spreads chocolate” is displayed while a hand applies chocolate spread to different foods.

First, an adult woman with dark curly hair bites into a small piece of bread or toast covered with chocolate spread. On the right, a hand spreads chocolate onto a similar small piece of bread using a knife above an open Hershey’s Spreads jar.

Next, a young girl is shown eating a small round item with chocolate spread, while the right side again shows a knife spreading chocolate onto a similar small item above the jar.

Then, a blond boy eats what appears to be a waffle or cracker with chocolate spread. On the right, a hand spreads chocolate onto a square waffle-like piece.

Next, an adult man with a beard bites into a pretzel or loop-shaped snack dipped in chocolate. On the right, a hand dips or coats a similar pretzel-shaped item in chocolate above the open jar.

Then, a young man eats a light-colored chip or cracker. On the right, a hand spreads chocolate onto a greenish slice or wedge-shaped snack with a knife above the same Hershey’s Spreads jar.

The commercial then cuts to a close-up of flowing or rippling chocolate with the word “HERSHEY’S” formed prominently in dark raised letters. This transitions into close-up product shots of jars. A jar labeled “HERSHEY’S spreads chocolate” fills the frame, with a yellow “NEW!” tag on the label and imagery including a strawberry and chocolate-covered food.

The final section presents multiple jars on a clean white surface. The camera or product arrangement shifts between jars, showing several flavors. The central product display includes three jars: one labeled “chocolate with almond,” one larger central jar labeled “chocolate with hazelnut,” and one labeled “chocolate.” All have silver lids, dark brown jars, large white “HERSHEY’S” branding, and yellow “NEW!” label markers. The sequence ends with continuing product shots of the jars sliding or shifting across the white background.
